title: sambucus racemosa var. stenophylla
intro: Sambucus racemosa var. stenophylla, also known as the narrow-leaved elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral compounds, making it effective in treating colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa var. stenophylla include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and boosting the immune system, while its berries are also used to treat digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
